reviews.review_text:
I really want to like this game. God knows I've spent enough time in it but I just cant recommend it.

First things, the good stuff:

It's very very pretty. Walking along a beach at dawn/dusk or sailing the Aegean during a storm can be genuinely stunning at times and reaching a viewpoint almost always comes with an impressive view of the world. And the world is massive. People criticise Ubisoft open worlds a lot but they do have a talent for it and the little details I feel often go overlooked. Those details are here.

There are places where the atmosphere is pretty good. Going for a stroll through the countryside or the city has the ambience right.

Historically it's not perfect of course and I know some people get very bothered by any inaccuracies but I'm less concerned about that. Things are broadly correct and it's enough for me, it's a game not a textbook.

The soundtrack is one of the better in the series. Although I feel it uses its weakest tracks the most frequently which is a bit disappointing.

The story is fine. That's all. Kassandra is pretty likeable as a main character (I would strongly advise choosing her over Alexios to play as).  Though it has pacing problems I'll save for later.

Now the bad, of which there is a lot:

Notice how all the stuff I praised essentially boiled down to aesthetics? That's because the game world falls apart somewhat when you actually have to interact with it. 

The vast majority of npcs feel like cartoon characters. I can count on one hand the amount of characters that feel like real people, and the voice acting and models don't help. Voice work is generally over the top and feels like a bad local theatre performance. There's weirdly few face/hair models for npc's so everyone quickly starts looking the same. So much so that I was convinced for much of the game that two characters were actually the same person and it would be a plot twist. It was not.

The games biggest crime, which infects everything else, is the levelling system. It is terrible and every part of the game suffers for it. Enemies level up along side you. This means that you never actually get stronger as the game progresses. You can be an end-game level 50 demigod but those regular town guards will still be just as difficult as they were at level 1, because whenever you level up they just follow you. 

The worst part of this is enemies are insane damage sponges. This isn't much of a problem early game but it becomes very frustrating later on. There is a lot of combat in this game and whilst the combat system itself is okay (it's doing a worse version of the fromsoft thing) it's exhausting after a while. Normal infantry taking multiple full combos to take down is bad enough when there's only a few of them but this game likes throwing armies at you. Enemy officers or rival mercenaries can take minutes of hacking away at. Some of the actual bossfights have health bars that would make Darkeater Midir blush. Unfortunately the combat isn't good enough to justify all engagements taking so dang long. It lacks the engagement of a fromsoft game and it doesn't make the game more challenging just more tedious. You will figure out enemy patterns very quickly but then have to spend twenty minutes chipping away at them. And that's if you lucky and the ai doesn't bug out and make all the enemies attack you at the same time (this happened to me a lot and would consistently kill me immediately).

Another side effect of this is that stealth becomes essentially useless. When only around 1 in 3 enemies can be reliably stealth killed (everyone else it just does a bonus damage attack and then alerts everyone around)  you stop bothering.  Enemies detect you immediately after the slightest glance. It feels like the stealth system is only here at all out of obligation as the game forces you into combat every chance it gets. The only times I did stealth were when I was so sick of the combat that I was avoiding it at all costs.

The levelling is also what destroys the story pacing. Areas/quests in the game have recommended levels, and trying to do anything more than one or two levels higher than you are is essentially impossible for the normal player. Main quests have an annoying habit of jumping recommended levels. You'll finish a level 18 mission and find that the next one is level 22. This means a good three or more hours of sidequesting to get up to scratch. This would be less painful if the sidequests were not terrible. There's a handful of good ones but this game must have over a thousand side quests but almost all of them are identical. Go to this place, kill enemies, come back. Most of them are clearly procedurally generated as the dialogue is very generic and often reused. And it's dialogue you'll be hearing a lot as levelling up is a very slow process and you'll be doing a huge amount of these quests.

This is made even worse by the amount of story quests that are essentially side quests. Frequently I'd finally be ready to do the next story mission. I'd go to the related character and they'd basically say "your next mission is to go to these three random people on other sides of the world from each other and complete a quest chain for them that doesn't link to the plot at all. After that come back and I'll tell you something you already knew". 

Seriously, one main quest mission had me speak to this kid about finding a key to a door. The kid says he doesn't know but sends me to see three other people and ask them. Each of those people I had to do a random quest for before they talk to me. After doing all three they tell me the kid has the key. I go back to the kid and he says "oh yeah this key sorry I forgot about that" and opens the door. The game played it off as a joke, like I was meant to say "oh you little rascal" but this took over two hours. I was furious. This is the worst kind of padding and everything in the game is like this. Everything is dragged out to the point of becoming miserable.

The game constantly teases you with greatness but every time it seems like something cool was about to happen the levelling system slams on the breaks and sends you off for several hours of nothing. Then when you finally do get to the cool bit, you realise it isn't cool. It's another of the same combat mission you've done dozens of times. 

The DLC. Gotta admit I haven't finished either. I got hours into both and realised it was just more of the same. Legacy of the First Blade is boring and forces you into a marriage and kids that was so out of character it felt like Kassandra was being violated in a weird way. I was actually kinda excited about Atlantis until I got there and a guy immediately sent me off to see three people who each had a series of unrelated combat jobs for me. A slightly depressive wave came over me as I saw that it was just going to be another twenty hours of the same thing. I just closed the game. I was done with the damage sponges. I was done with the fort infiltrations. I was done with doing the same things over and over and expecting the game to finally come into its own. 

That is Odyssey's greatest skill. It keeps you playing. It is fantastic at making you think that it's going to get good any minute now. Just a few more quests. Just one or two more levels and I can do that thing that sounded cool. No. It's more of the same. I am tired of Odyssey but I don't hate it. Somewhere in here there is something great. Remove the levelling system, cut out all the padding and tighten up some of the writing and there's a great game here. Unfortunately playing it really does feel like a second job.

Will I keep playing? Honestly probably yes. Should you? If you like Valhalla then maybe you'll have a blast. If not, just keep in mind the sunk cost fallacy. Don't be me and keep going out of obligation.

Such a beautiful game, loved it
Beautifully massive map to explore so many things to do
The combat is good it was fun especially the legendary animals. Killing the cults is fun and satisfying.
The music is good suits very well
The abilities are very good and fun. Personally I loved the skill where you can jump from any height and get no damage and the skill where you do a stealth kill the body disappears and some bow and arrow skills. Additionally you can enhance them in the fate of atlantis.
The game is really great but by the time you complete 75% of the game it gets tedious af but you can get happy ending. 
Then you start legacy dlc it was nice but just weird and unexpected af
THEN COME THE FATE OF ATLANTIS ABSOLUTELY FKING GORGEOUS!

ALthough the story felt fukd up to me
SPOILER ALERT
You start your journey to find your family just to find your father is your step father and he adopted a son. You end up meeting your step brother, puppet of cult, convince him for the happy ending. you can have them on your ship. Then you end up finding your biological father who is an immortal noob.
Then comes the legacy all of a sudden you are married and have a kid just to have your partner die. saw it coming miles away. this whole dlc felt creepy.
The fate of atlantis is just amazing but you see messed up stuff

In over 25 years of gaming, Odyssey is in my top 5 favorite games ever.  I loved it.  Perfect blend of action/adventure with RPG elements.  All aspects of the game are super high quality: the writing, dialog, action, artwork, animation, cutscenes, and UI.  It's very immersing and included many memorable emotional scenes.  The map is immense.  The volume of quests is immense, and most of the Gold-bordered side quests are worth doing.  The main story quests are fun and do have some meaningful choices.

In addition to main and side quests, there are several other features to keep you busy, including a huge tree of Cultists to discover and defeat, tons of challenging unique enemy Mercenaries to defeat (I defeated almost 200), and simple romance options (nothing more than one night stands though :)).  Crafting is done at a blacksmith, super easy and effective.  Inventory management is minimal, way easier than any Bethesda game.  

The skill tree is cool, being separated into Hunter, Warrior, and Assassin abilities, with melee and ranged wheels to set up hotkeys during combat.  It's also great that you can re-spec at any time for a nominal cost, which lets you experiment and later discard skills that don't fit your playstyle.

After finishing the main story and the Cultists side story, the ending epilogue questline called A Great Escape was a perfect finisher.  It tied up a couple of the main friendships formed during the game, and was an adventure in and of itself.  A great lead-in to the final credits (and don't miss the Marvel-like post-credit scene!).

i dont understand the hate towards this piece of art, prob people just dont know how to enjoy games anymore. this game is just beautifull. people are crazy hating on this game just because the title is assassin creed. they prob not enjoying it because they only play linear game and dont know how to enjoy open world rpg game. this game is overhated maybe the ending is not what i expected to be that cliche i expected more drama and emotion in the ending. anyway i still exploring the dlc and this is my comfort game i cant wait to play ac valhalla cuz i really like vikings.
Basic Ubisoft open world game set in ancient Greek. Good ship combat, lots of side quests and big boss fights. The overall story of the base game is decent.
The game is far too long; it just gets tedious and very boring after a while. The story isn't interesting. I don't recommend buying it.

The story for this game is truly the work of countless talented writers who put the likes of Dostoyevski, Shakespear and Hemingway to shame. The game's lore is immaculate and really makes you feel like a Greek. Another home run by Ubisoft, cinematic masters of whom Quentin Taranmido is envious beyond belief. The depth of the characters will leave you wanting more, the amazing plot points of shoving eyes down a goat's ass will leave you sobbing and crying on your desk.

The gameplay leaves a hole in your heart, begging to be filled by more. The snappy and powerful combat truly makes you feel like a sweaty, strong man which many of Ubisoft's writers are. The option of having intercourse with almost every side character possible is also an amazing self insert from the Ubisoft dev, the stalions that they are.

This game truly is a masterpiece

Less of a traditional stealth game, and more of an incredibly violent, breathtaking vacation to Ancient Greece.

If you are looking for the most visually stunning and vibrant game in the entire franchise, this is undeniably it. Assassin's Creed Odyssey throws away the drab, grey color palettes of older titles and replaces them with a breathtakingly gorgeous Mediterranean aesthetic. The water is a sparkling turquoise, the forests are lush and vibrant, and the towering statues of the gods look phenomenal against the sun-drenched landscapes. It is a visual masterpiece, and you will likely spend your first dozen hours just sailing your ship around the Aegean Sea to take screenshots of the unbelievable scenery.

What truly sets Odyssey apart from the rest of the series is how brilliantly it embraces Greek mythology. You aren't just a sneaky assassin hiding in haystacks; you are essentially a demigod. The game seamlessly blends historical settings—where you can casually debate philosophy with Sokrates—with epic, fantastical elements. Hunting down legendary mythical beasts like the Minotaur, Medusa, or a Cyclops feels incredibly rewarding and fits perfectly into the larger-than-life heroic fantasy the game is trying to sell.

You should know going in that this game is astronomically, unapologetically vast. The map is unimaginably huge, filled to the brim with quests, mercenaries to hunt, and islands to explore. While some might find that scale intimidating, if you love getting lost in a beautiful, rich world, it’s exactly what you want. It might stray far from the traditional Assassin's Creed roots, but as a grand, mythical, open-world RPG, it is an absolute triumph.

10/10 — 100 hours in, and I am still kicking people off cliffs just to yell "This is Sparta!"

I'm not sure how to feel about Odyssey so far. Much like origins it seems to have strayed a little far from the core of what Assassin's Creed is about, yet is still very fun.

I would say that this is probably not a good Assassin's Creed game, but in it's own right a fun open world game. I would recommend it as I think there is a lot of exploration and fun to be had here, but I wouldn't pick it up if your looking for a classic Assassin's Creed game, because in that regard, it's not good.

This is probably my favorite Assassin's Creed game. It had a good blend of straightforward fighting and stealth assassination options. The Greek world felt alive. The game was long enough to feel epic in scope, but it didn't feel overly dragged out (as compared to Valhalla).

My view of this game is neutral but considering this game is an assassins creed game that is fairly below expectation. First off this game does not play like an assassins creed game, this is essentially the second entry in the warrior creed games. My first major problem with this game is every enemy is way to tanky. The style of combat in this game has been outdated since it came out, the boring skill wheel system and requiring a huge amount of light attacks is boring. The main issue I have with this game is the mercenary system which feels like a poor attempt at the orc captains from shadow of Mordor. Some how all the way through the game over leveled mercs would spawn on me and hunt me down with their apparent gps locator. The mercs were not nearly interesting enough to be this constantly present, and they were so bad one literally spawned in my final fight with Deimos. Also why make us fight Deimos twice, its literally the same fight twice. He also is a really boring fight, using invincibility as a difficulty mechanic sucks. Then there is hunting the cultists, why not add a cutscene when you kill them, the thing done in all previous games. Hunting the cult didn't feel special because there was not interaction with them. Then there is the story, it felt like a series of plot points not really like a story, Bethesda level writing. The level scaling system was way to balanced, you pretty much always require the same number of hits to kill an enemy, no build really scales well. Leveling gear just becomes a grindy chore, this game really likes making you grind for everything. Missions that could be singular missions are made into three just because. This game is a completionists nightmare, there are basically endless side things to do for no reason. I actually really liked the parkour in this game, it was very clean. The side quests and romance systems were nice. I didn't find the pointless decisions to be a fun mechanic, don't give the player choices if they don't have a tangible effect on outcome. The ship combat was improved from origins but fell short of black flag, it was pretty fun. The stupid tablets being an upgrade material was terrible. In all the game was fun for a playthrough but this is so far the only assassins creed I would not replay just because the level of grindyness needed to do anything.

I haven't played many AC games in my life, so I can't really say if it's a good Assassin's Creed. But I did play my share of RPG games, and I tell you this is a GREAT RPG.

We can say a lot of things about Ubisoft, but when they put love in a game, they put LOVE in the said game. Everything about AC Odyssey works well, the gameplay is smooth and varied, the music is incredible, and don't get me started on the world design. Every single place where you could stop could be worth a screenshot.

Regarding the gameplay, you get light and heavy attacks, dodges, and parries. 5 melee weapon types, a ranged weapon, a stealth system, and those 3 categories have their own skill trees.

You're not stuck with one way of playing, you can specialize your character on one thing or balance everything.

For the difficulty settings, I played most of the game on Nightmare then went on easy to clean the rest of the achievements I missed, so, having played on both of the sides of the difficulty spectrum of this game, I can tell that it's satisfying to play no matter which one you choose, I didn't really feel a change in the amount of HP the enemy had, the only thing that really seemed to change is the amount of damage you take and the level scaling of the enemies (which you can all change at any time during the game, difficulty AND scaling). So it's more a matter of strategy and making fewer mistakes if you play on higher difficulty, which I enjoyed.

The story is great, it's pretty hard to talk about it without spoiling anything, so I'll just try to convey my feelings when I played it.

You have a nice story to follow and characters you meet and learn to love, most of whom come back multiple times throughout the game, and they come back even more during the side quests, and people usually look down on RPG side quests, but here in ACO, it's probably one of its strongest points. They're either the funniest thing ever or totally heartbreaking.

There isn't a single side quest created by the devs that I felt was meaningless. And what I mean by "created by the devs" is that you also get randomly generated quests where it's indeed, go kill that, go fetch that (the random quests are usually grey or on the message board, and the real side quests are the ones with the yellow indicators)... But if it's a real side quest, created by the devs, they are VERY well made, and most of them are usually part of a side-questline, which reinforces even more the feeling of actually doing something, learning about character, and feeling satisfied when you complete it entirely.

The MC is amazing. I only played with Alexios, so I don't really know about Kassandra, but I loved his attitude, the dialog options, and the way he reacts to certain situations. The character really feels alive, and it helps a lot for the immersion.

Talking about the immersion, the world is pretty lively, between wild animals, camps, forts, villages, and cities, which are always flowing with people who are constantly creating background noises. It's to the point where it could almost feel relaxing to go through some places, even with how loud some are; that's how good the sound design is for this game. So the game doesn't feel empty, but it's not filled with so many things that it would feel overwhelming, they really found the good middle ground.

To complete the main game, the two DLCs, and the achievements, it took me +130 hours. It may be different from the usual Assassin's Creed game, but if you take it as an RPG, you'll have an amazing time.

this is my favorite AC game of all time.
2nd would be Black Flag.
I just hope Ubisoft will go back to making a real game. not a Woke bloke.

Graphics? Great. 
Story? Eh. 
Gameplay? Eh. 
RPG Elements? Eh. 
Progression? Eh. 
Voice acting? Eh. 
Itemization? Eh. 
Pacing? Eh. 
Constant crashes. 
Playing for extended amount of time causes it to stutter and eventually needs to be restarted.
Good if on sale for like 20 bucks. Otherwise, just buy Black Flag.

Assassin's Creed Odyssey is one I have a lot of thoughts on. 

Initially I was craving something to really sink my teeth into. The ancient Greek setting really spoke to me. I spent 42 hours in this game and a part of me really wanted to go further and finish some of the other campaigns. Around 20 to 25 hours I was actually having a pretty good time with the remaining time being frustration and tedious busy work. 

The beginning of the game is set up wonderfully, the story, the characters, the wonder what the world might bring. It was honestly alot of fun. It really slowly introduces you to what the game has in store for you. It starts off with great pacing and slowly bringing you to location to location. Then you get to Greater Athens. The area ends in a mission that is a 3 parter that has you tediously going to 3 different areas that given, are relatively close. However this is where I first started to feel the length of this game. I had to go to these places to look for someone but they were fairly far apart and a lot of distance to cover and it felt like I was just running forever. Where the game starts you off area to area, all of a sudden I was running all over the place and it really started to wear on me because the game is just so big. 

Then the perfect thing happened, the game pushed me to a different area of the game that happened to be smaller and a ton of fun to explore. I actually kind of fell in love with the naval combat a little bit. 

THEN the game brings you to Sparta, which cool. However they are now having the main missions be across the map from you and from this point on you are running all over this massive map and the tedious and repetative nature of the game becomes so obvious that most of my joy was seeped from me.

I do not mind a game being massive, it's what I was looking for. The combat in the game is annoying. The enemies are sponges and it gets worse and worse as the game goes on. The bounty system, which I thought was kind of neat to start showed its annoyances. I would be fighting a mission boss or enemy and a bounty hunter or two would come in as well. I would be fighting a mercenary and then the citizens would start attacking me, or the local guards. This got so frusterating. 

Then we have the weird monotization implementations. You can buy an XP boost, which makes me think that they slowed leveling down on purpose. Their are a million fluff side missions that are timed. If I wanted to play an MMO, I would be playing an MMO. This all felt so weird and gross in a singleplayer game. First time I am truly seeing this and it makes me happy that I haven't seen this stuff in other games. 

I enjoyed this game but its shortcomings over shadowed the things this game does right. If the combat system was actually interesting, I would be higher on it but it got stale pretty fast.
Not into it. Feels like an unfulfilling time-sink. Had the same feeling with Valhalla. Play the old ones.
300 hours!!! Damn, and I still wanna play more. There is so much in this game to explore.
The game is good, but the forced ubisoft launcher is garbage - it's either not launching, forcing often file verification or not syncing saves to cloud, which lost me few hours of gameplay.

The game is a great 8 hour open world adventure, right until [spoiler] you talk to Elpenor after dealing with Nikolaos. [/spoiler]
After that, the game's story is regularly forced to pay alimony to the open world by means of your attention. What is, at it's core, a decent story is watered down by needlessly doing what should be sidequests dealing with random NPCs in seemingly arbitrary parts of the world.

The game is at its best when it narrows its scope to smaller areas like Kephallonia [spoiler] or later Mykonos and Naxos. [/spoiler] 
Ironically (and, I think, terribly for an open world game), the game is at its worst when it wants you to find a new location, because you either get the waypoint or you get a rough description that is almost guaranteed to get you in sight of your target. That means you'll usually do very little exploring and instead spend your time looking at the map, placing a marker and waiting for your horse's autopilot to get you there.
That also means that the game will suddenly end after travelling and teleporting between three to five known locations. If you, like me, have grown tired of the slog the game has been by then, you will likely be skipping or ignoring most dialogue and be absolutely blindsided by the "Finish Chapter 9 + Epilogue" achievement at a point that seems not at all climactic enough to justify it.

The game's systems all work competently, ship combat is fun, the skill tree grants meaningful choices and the core gameplay is fun enough not to annoy you at the end of the game. The level enforcements sometimes feel like arbitrary barriers where the game just stops you from progressing the story by giving even the most minute enemies the ability to oneshot you.

If you have finished the prologue, you have seen the best the game has to offer. It will never be as good as Kephallonia again.

Well considering so many people calling it too much grindy, I wouldn't disagree on that. But I would say it is a fun and worthy grind. Conquest battles to doing the side missions, I really did enjoy it. Now for the main storyline I played as Kassandra and it was beautifully made. I absolutely loved the character development of Kassandra and how well the other chharacters fits in the storyline. 100% would recommend to play it.
Really fun game. Just forget that this is supossed to be an entry in the Assasins Creed franchise and you will be good to go
Fire game with giant world map and great graphics.

World is fantastic. For lovers of the Greek mythology and ancient greece, perfect. 

Runs smooth AF.

The only downside is, that it is a Ubisoft formula game and at times quests will feel repetative (that's mostly cause it so many of them everywhere). But even that can be overlooked, at least IMO, because the world feels alive enough. 

The song being sung on the ship are actual songs from the era. Most of the places you can visit are or used to be real places. Amazing.


8/10

If you want a game with what feels like endless content this is the game. It took me 156 hours to 100% it with all the DLCs and some of those hours are afk but still. Some games nowadays you dont even get a third of that time before you 100% it and they cost more money.
i have an obsession with ancient greek furniture and thats why i bought this but i love the actual game too. 10/10

A faithful, yet fun retelling and adaptation of Greek mythology and history. Gameplay mechanics are not perfect, but the world, characters and atmosphere of Assassins Creed Odyssey are worth both the temporal and financial investments. I have a soft spot for Greece and can't keep myself away from this game.
really good game. if you like to explore the map, do side quests and kill people this game is for you

Honestly a great game. I really enjoyed the main storyline and the DLCs. My only wish is that for the exploration mode, it was more vague, because in reality it's just the same but without the marker.

This plays more like an annoyingly difficult hack and slash rather than a stelth game.
While that's fun at first, getting caught in long battles where an entire fort of soldiers 
stand between you and your goal for the 40th time, is just...annoying.

This game lies to you. It is in no way an assassin's creed game. Most things about it sucks, its stealth system is so undercooked you'll wonder why they bothered in the first place. And it has the gall to ask you to do some missions with stealth.

You might say, okay, its stealth sucks, so the combat is good, right? Wrong. The combat is slow and boring, the enemies are damage sponges, no matter where you go, they can only ever be 4 levels behind you (and that's if you tweak the settings.) So some civilians in the starting area can be as spongy as guards in a late game area.

If you get into a fight in a crowded place, congrats, the civilians now want to join in and kill you, if you kill them, now you have 4 mercenaries to deal with in addition to whomever you were fighting, now it's 5 minutes later, the ground is covered in bodies and you're finally done button mashing, because that's all the depth the combat system has. 

The textures still look pretty bad on the highest settings, I know it has a different art style, but it makes it feel like you're just playing a game, for me, it ruined immersion. The characters feel like cartoon characters, the voice acting is bad, the animations are bad. I probably only saw 4-5 cutscenes in my 127 hours of playing.

The writing is also terrible. People say this game has great sidequests. Where? Most of the content in this game is fetch quests, go here - bring this back to me. Go here - kill this guy in this fort. That, for 120+ hours. 

The main character also sucks, there's no character arc, no development, no moral compass or a set of values. Oh, side quest giver, you want me to kill this random woman because you're a politician and her only crime was sleeping with you and it'd look bad that you cheated? Sure, as long as I get my drachmae. That's the sort of person I'm playing but then I'm supposed to turn around and judge others for being killers? Great.

Also, the controls are absolutely terrible, the running animation makes it look like a toddler who just learned how to jog, there's no sprint button, they just do it automatically, the parkour is also god awful, there's no depth, you just press shift and you can climb on any cliff you want. You can get stuck ON FLAT GROUND. And that happens a lot too. And even though you can climb on anything, climbing on things you actually want to climb on still doesn't work properly. Oh Kassandra can climb on the steepest statue or cliff you've seen but this tiny little ledge? No shot she can make it up there.

Don't play this awful game.
Great just the story ended very unexpectedly and abruptly
Good game but sometimes ubisoft launcher won't work or sometimes crashes
This isn't just a game, its a journey. Ridiculously fun with boatloads of content. Frankly, I'm hard pressed to say anything remotely negative.

People will say its too long... 
They are just scared to comitt...
Game is just too beautiful.... Better than Valhalla
Also about hidden blade. So the game takes place before they discover the hidden blade technique so the spear acts like hidden blade during this era...

My best AC game, beautiful landscape immersing Greek world with tons of activities and adventure. I love Greek mythology and love traveling, this game is for me! 

Not too get into the Combat system at first, but now the "Sparta Way" is my favorite playstyle now.

i really enjoyed this game. the only complain i could have is when you try to go stealth and try to kill some enemies with the bow before hand, you have to invested in bow skills. so i barely used the bow after realising this and focued on close combat and stealth kills. game was a blast.
my favorite AC since at least black flag. the naval combat in this one is pretty good too. AC games made after this one havent clicked for me.

I must have more than 400h on this game now counting the hundred of hours I've put on the free PC version I got from sketchy sites. Old now, but still have the beautiful white beaches you can walk on and hear the waves breaking, actually this is the main reason I'm giving this game a positive, anything else is UBISOFT. I live near beaches rn but damn these Greek beaches are on another level bruh.

This game has more going for it than many players give it credit for.  This is very evident through the excellent prologue which introduces you, the player, into this cyber-enhanced sideways glance into ancient history.

An additional deft touch is the on-ship interplay between Kassandra and Barnabas and any passenger.  Indeed, any interplay during quests is remarkably well done (in my humble opinion).

It is too easy to overlook the joy of the game and its stunning scenery by fast-travelling too much.  Please, do yourself a favour, and horse-ride and ship travel as the mood takes you.  Soak in the sights and sounds of the game -- don't rush through this greek world -- and it becomes remarkably chill and relaxing to play when you do.

While it's not exactly the "Assassin" style game, it is one of their best in the series.

AC Odyssey hits on the story, design, combat, and Greek myth. The DLC is well thought out and was surprisingly good so far, I have yet to touch on the Atlantis DLC. 

9.5/10. So worth, and very replayable.
Great game, gooners with gooner mods post photos, fun for the whole fam, and a quick way to get a divorce. doesn't matter that you wont get the kids, this game is your life now anyways!!!
awesome graphics and story line. I have played this game thru going on my 3rd time and haven't gotten tired of it. 10/10 would recommend !

Took me a while to really get into it, but once I did I couldn't put it down. Even after finishing the main story I just kept going. Fortunately its not like most OW games where the missions are done. I still had tons to do and I found that stuff way more engaging than the main story.
I wanted to like this game, because I LOVED Origins. But it's kind of boring. Very pretty, but boring.

This game is a brutal grindfest. Everything is fine at first, but later on you have to grind for hours just to level up, and the game is all about paying money if you want to get ahead. Still, I liked the story, and the Ancient Greece atmosphere is really good.

Gameplay is almost the same as Origins, enjoyable enough. even though visual effects and teleportations make negative visual effect. But by the shire size of the game it'll become a routine. just doin any two-three skills combo to clear every area.
Out of Animus talking heads become even more obnoxious than in previous game. hopefully there not a lot of em in gameplay.

But the thing that make this game pretty bad is the Dialogs. they are just horrible. the acting jump from line to line, the pronunciations of names and places can change during the same dialog. animations don't sync with replicas. The situation is better in additional quests from DLC, but most of the game it's annoyingly bad

This game perfectly balances graphics, gameplay, difficulty, storytelling and adventure. None is perfect (arguably graphics may be) but all are at high standard. If you like action and challenge, you may have it. If you prefer adventure with ease, you can have it. Anything in between. Most likely obtainable as well.

It may become repetitive at times, there is a bit of grind, but the game is great.
One of the best AC games in recent memory. Very detailed open world, nice combat system, only problem is that the graphics are not really optimized for PC and FPS can jump quite a bit sometimes.

This game is really, really good. It does a lot of things right and you will enjoy your time if you like to play big open world.

It's a fun RPG set in the ancient greek world during the war between Sparta and Athen.
You have good variety on the build side so you can really have fun experimenting the build that you like to play.
The boat gameplay is also very well done and I had a lot of fun playing with it.

However, don't treat it like your standard assassin's creed game. The story (without the DLC at least) doesn't have any mention of the assassin's in it and the stealth tools are... non existent.

If you want to do everything on the map, be weary that it will take you a long time since this game is massive. You will have to be prepeared to encounter some repetition and staleness too.

Visuals and vista make this game. The vibe and story are brilliant, but oh boy the combat. If the combat was anywhere near half decent it would have been a 9/10 game easily, but instead its a 6 at best. Sadge

I started playing AC Origins and LOVED it... AC Odyssey has all the good bits of Origins, plus more. The game is also huge - I'm barely any of the way through (mainly because I am always getting distracted on side quests), but it is a great way to spend a few hours if I don't really feel like playing the main storyline. I also like that there are different ways to play and it is a little bit of a choose your own. The worst bit, as always, is the actual Animus bit with Layla (I know it's the whole thing, but it is boring), but at least it is easy to skip.
My Fav Assassins Creed Game .. also the first assassins creed game i ever played in my life :)

It has been my absolute dream to own the official Steam release of this game. After 7 long years of waiting, I finally grabbed it on sale, and I swear I am physically breathing better now. The itch has been scratched, and it was worth the wait.
beautiful scenery, decent gameplay to keep you engaged for hours.

A fun RPG, although a lot of features do feel a bit 'dumbed down' to cater to the wider audience. The fact that you either spec into combat OR assassin when most of the time you're being forced into combat anyway is kind of a massive letdown. This game also has a bit too much downtime, I think mainly because the map is far too big for the amount of content within.

Assassin's Creed Odyssey is a good game, just not a good Assassin's Creed Game. Just remove 'Assassin's Creed' from the box and maybe it would've been received much better on release.

To me, this is the absolute best Assassin's Creed game of all time. I've sunk countless hours into it, and I'm fully committed to finishing the main story along with all the DLCs. Everything about it: the narrative, the weapon variety, and the character development. If you have any love for Greek literature and mythology, this is a absolute must-play.

7/10.
The story is mostly meh, carried by DLCs, especially legacy of the first blade.
The combat is ok, but very reliant on skills that take a while to fully upgrade.
I hate the damn horse.
Atlantis DLC was the most beautiful thing in the entire franchise in my opinion, and overall the world looks great, however sometimes it can feel way to big.
I like the ship combat, ship upgrades feel meaningful but they are very expensive.
The ending of the base game is HORRIBLE. DLC endings are way better. If u don't have money for the dlcs + base game don't bother buying it at all imo.
Overall, i really enjoyed it, Greece is beautiful, Kassandra voice acting is great, the combat is pretty fun, especially with the assassin build.

Story Line is Great with either Character
Allows for multiple easy playthroughs (same story different emotions and styles)

bitmeyen oyun yapmışlar bu ne aq. 75 saatte ana hikaye kollarından birini bitirdim doydum kalanını yapasım yok. Bu nedir yav zaten yaptığın şeyler de birbirinin aynısı. Lots of complaints about the game, but hell, I finished it twice. Love it overall!
Great game in its own right . is it a true assassins creed ? , probably not ..but as RPG's go this is 1 of the best.

The bounty hunters are impossible to get past and show up during the middle of missions so much that I just quit. There is no chance of you killing them and definitely no right way to play this game. I love Assassin's Creed and own all of them so far, but this just can't pass as playable in my book.
bad ac but great game itself. grapichs are beautiful and combat is fun too
Absolutely an underrated gem, best graphics and desired gameplay according to your style either assassination or hand to hand combat

one of the best games i ever played ! i loved the story, the graphics, GREECE is MAGIC, this is obviously the best AC game i ever played, and overall a really great content with +100 hours, and guess what ? i got it with all DLC for 20 bucks only, very happy with that, now i ended the main story after 55 hours, i still have 2 DLC to play. 
Note : this is the third time i play the game (played it on xbox game pass) thought it deserves a buy on steam, and yep ! love it. thank you for this one UBISOFT !
Great game. Love the music and the story.

Amazing storyline and graphics, aswell as the classes, music, and combat. Having a lot of trouble levelling up to the right level for where the game tells me to go. I'm currently in athens (the storyline told me to go there) and I'm 3-4 levels behind what all the quests and every soldier/enemy is here.

With each Assassin’s Creed game I play, I appreciate Odyssey even more. The story, atmosphere, world design, and characters were all great. The combat was also satisfying in terms of overall feel, mechanics, and variety.

I know a lot of people (still???) hate the newer games’ combat and especially complain that “there is no stealth,” but honestly, I think the stealth in this "RPG Assasins Creed' " is better than the older games' stealth.

I get the nostalgia for the old titles - they had their own unique “fake stealth” feeling - but when you replay them today, their flaws become a lot more noticeable.

Also, Odyssey has my favorite *ahem* protagonist in the entire series: Kassandra, my beloved <3

(Yeah, yeah, Ezio is great too, and I love him as well… but I said what I said.)

Assassin's creed odyssey is a beautiful open-world game set in Ancient Greece with fun combat, great exploration, and an engaging story. Although the map can feel a bit too large at times, it’s still an exciting and enjoyable RPG adventure. Have fun!

The graphics are really nice, the landscapes in this game are stunning, really enjoyable gameplay and story. 
Do not recommend to grind the forts and camps for the firts time you play with it, try balancing it as the main story goes, else it will get really boring and you will leave the game after the first 20 hours.

I genuinely love this game, but would I really recommend this for someone to play? no. The story is half-baked with few good parts. The combat is very simply and only fun when you completely remove an aspect of it to challenge yourself against a bunch of NPC's, Spartan/Athenian guards and 5 mercenaries all coming at you at once.
Very underrated. Doesn't deserved the hate it gets. Do yourself a favor and give it a chance and play the game with an open mind. SOlid game 9.5/10.

Wish I could leave a Mixed review. As an assassins creed game...it's awful. There is very little assassins creed in this assassins creed game. As a Greek open world rpg..its decent. The world is beautiful and the locations are easily the strongest point of the game, Athens and Sparta esspecially. The combat is..fine. The skill tree is good enough. But the grind is awful. You get so little from quests that I do not suggest anyone play it without something to increase exp and coin gains. Enemies have giant health bars and after a certain point, most enemies just...wont take knockback, making one of the most satisfying moves to use, the Spartan Kick, next to worthless. Also the story just...ends. Like you get to the final fight, can convince them not to fight and then..it just ends. No credits, just "Hey, you can play NG+ now". I'd give this like a 6/10 if you like Greek mythos and like a 4/10 if not.

10/10

i am not usually very big on the AC series - i tried AC 2, origins etc and they were good, don't get me wrong, but this game absolutely encapsulates anything you could want out of an open world rpg. 
do note, i am slightly biased, being an absolute classics nerd, but i want you to know just how important the attention to detail is - they included specific friezes and their depictions in the parthenon! very impressive.
i completed this on xbox and it took around 130 hours to do, so definitely expect a long grind. one thing i would say is that because the map is so enormous, it does take many hours (probably close to 70) just to complete all of the sections and their smaller missions/areas etc, so the way i did it on my second run was to mix completion of areas with the main story - what way you won't be done with the story and have a whole load of the map still left to complete. it also keeps it really engaging and fun.
the outfits are top notch - and customisable appearance-wise which is a new feature that i would definitely recommend trying.
and the scenery alone is a 10/10.
a truly masterful game, and one that i haven't been able to stay away from even having completed multiple times.
The main game was decent, and so was the legacy of the first blade. I found the fate of Atlantis super repetitive and boring.
This is the first game i've played in the AC franchise and it's worth every dollar spent. This game is highly addicting and there's always something to keep you engaged.

8.5/10. On one hand this game is an amazing journey in a beautiful, immersive world with very enjoyable RPG elements, but on the other hand it does drag quite a bit, especially with the DLC.

The story is fun and I liked it, but not great, I found the stealth to actually be very fun, the on-land combat was just button mashing at some point when I overlevelled by doing all the side stuff first, the ship-combat was very fun though and I also liked purchasing new upgrades for the ship.

The DLC for this game is fun but not a must.

This world is MASSIVE. It is super fun and so easy to get lost in the story and the world. It is beautiful and truly makes a great session for therapy. The combat is what you would expect, nothing crazy but does it really need to be? It is a AC game after all, the stealth in this game is really nice because you can clear out entire camps without being seen or even get what you need and get out without killing a single person. The soundtrack is choice, sometimes play without music just to listen to some good vibes. First time playing this and I am loving it, just enjoy the world and DO NOT try to 100% the game, there is so much content, just do what you want and you will love it.
this game was great af, glad i platinated this game right after ac origins. i would give this game a 9 out 10 if it wasnt for the animus stuff, overall 8.5/10
great game. more combat focused than other assassins creed games. feels like endless content, the map is huge and there are sooo many quests

An Entertaining Greek Odyssey, but Origins is the True Masterpiece
Rating: 7/10 (Good, but flawed)
After spending significant time with Assassin’s Creed Odyssey, I have deeply mixed feelings about the experience. While the game boasts an incredible setting and a captivating narrative, the fundamental gameplay loop suffers from heavy repetition, especially when compared to its phenomenal predecessor, Assassin’s Creed Origins.
The Problem with Exploration and Progression
My biggest grievance with Odyssey lies in its level design and exploration mechanics. Whenever you are tasked with entering a cave. Whether it is to hunt down treasure, loot a chest, or find an Ancient Tablet to earn ability points. The experience becomes incredibly monotonous. Every subterranean area follows the exact same architectural asset design and relies on identical tactical layouts.
The encounters are highly predictable; you crawl through the dark, deal with the exact same annoying snake placements, and dodge the same uninspired traps. It completely kills the thrill of discovery because once you have cleared two or three caves, you have essentially cleared them all. Gathering ability points feels more like a tedious chore than a rewarding progression system.
The Highlights: Map Freedom and Lore
That being said, the game does have some highly enjoyable mechanics. One of my absolute favorite features is the completely unlocked vertical mobility. Once you level up enough to negate fall damage, jumping off massive Greek cliffs without consequence feels incredibly liberating and fun.
Furthermore, if you are a fan of rich narrative writing, historical fiction, or Greek mythology, Odyssey delivers a great story. The political intrigue, the interactions with historical figures, and the looming presence of the Greek gods provide an excellent narrative backdrop that kept me engaged.
Why Origins Reigns Supreme
However, for me, Assassin’s Creed Origins is the superior game in every single category. In Origins, the gameplay feels fundamentally fresh and intentional. The environmental puzzles are brilliant, the ability progression mechanics are perfectly balanced, and the map feels uniquely handcrafted rather than mass-produced.
Most importantly, Origins excels at emotional immersion. The rich story and masterful writing make you truly connect with Bayek; you feel like you are that character, sharing in his grief and his quest for justice. In Odyssey, that deep emotional connection to the protagonist is noticeably weaker.
Final Verdict
If you are new to the modern RPG trilogy of this franchise, I highly recommend playing Odyssey first, and then moving on to Origins. Odyssey is a good game with a compelling story, but it simply does not hold a candle to the atmosphere, gameplay variety, and mechanical depth of Origins. Odyssey is worth playing for the plot, but Origins is the true masterpiece.
It's an interesting game. It has a lots of bug, but overall, it's better than MIrage, but not better than Origins.
Surprisingly good. Expected it to be mediocre. Combat is quite difficult in the beginning but gets better when you unlock more skills. And the environment is beautiful.

I am really loving this game. All of the ACs I have played thus far are good. But this one is amazing imho.

Pros:
-  Great graphics and capture of how Ancient Greece must have looked and felt to live in. It gives a very real worldish spin on Greek Mythology and lore. Instead of it feeling like a supernatural thriller action adventure RPG it feels more grounded in realism.
-  What I said above about the Mythology and Lore
-  I really like the action of combat; both melee and ranged. I like the customizability and free-style you can go about your character's build for a playthrough. I'm going a mixed combat style but not using poison.
-  Very large open sandbox type world!

Cons:
-  Honestly, my game has been bug-free and plays according to the way is expected. Solid programming in my opinion.
-  None others that I can think of.

Its alright. I dont mind the combat like some others do. The worst part of the rpg progression is that for some reason something 3 levels higher than you just obliterates you in one hit, so you never really feel like you're progressing. Otherwise, the story is fine? Dialog is pretty terrible at times. Choices dont match what it actually has you say. Classic stuff, really. DLC story is exceptionally bad. The world is really really good though. They dont do a great job of building it through story, but that's just classic AC. They have the luxury of leaning on existing mythology to do much of the building that they should be doing. Kinda lazy. Movement is pretty meh too, which is disappointing. Could be worse tho.

Charming visuals and atmosphere. open seas and endless landscapes. Anyway, all Assassin’s Creed games are good in their own way — some have their strengths, some their flaws. I’m a true admirer of the series, especially the older titles.

This game truly is incredible. It's the first of the Assassin's Creed RPGs I've played, and I was very hesitant with that change in direction. However, this game truly is phenomenal. It has a super satisying game loop. There are hundreds of interesting bases, forts and camps to beat. The story is strong enough to keep my interest and the progression is great. I truly loved my time beating the game.

Assassin's Creed Odyssey is quite a fun game, with a variety of relatively interesting activities across its beautifully rendered map of Greece, but is mired with the classic Ubisoft malaise that makes actually recommending it to anybody dishonest. My rating is more "I liked it" than "I think you should play it".

Overall rating: 6.8/10. Only buy on sale.

Long-ass review below.

---

This game has a LOT of content, and I mean that in both a literal and pejorative sense. The second of the three "really big open world" AC games, it borrows much from Origins - not only the UI, but also a variety of gameplay mechanics - which seems to have allowed the developers to shift attention to adding more to the game. As there's so much to cover, I will be as brief as possible for each point.

The Open World: On the graphics side, Greece is impressively rendered, and the game clearly knows how to create vistas. On the gameplay side, Greece has been expectedly uniformly shrunk down so that it's actually a playable space. This produces some noticeable discontinuities, such as the cities feeling a little small and the distances between them feeling a little narrow, but the design broadly succeeds. As always, the test of game design isn't so much *designing* an open world, it's putting stuff in it to do. The game has much more mixed results here, using a mixture of randomly generated radiant quests as well as human-designed quests, some of which are part of broader storylines (e.g. a questline where you hang out with Socrates). If going for full exploration, most of your time will be spent running between largely copypasted bandit/guard camps and killing/looting everybody there, as well as exploring little caves and shipwrecks. Most of the towns and cities have very little to do inside them, with only the major ones like Athens and Sparta getting human-added questlines. There's a series of subsystems that further populate the world, which I'll briefly list below:

- Mercenaries: Elite enemies that roam around, chasing you in response to you committing crimes. Oddly, you're rewarded quite highly for killing them, which means you're incentivized for being a little ♥♥♥♥ everywhere you go. You can pay a relatively small sum to make them go away for a while.

- Cultists: A story-relevant thing and so I won't go into much detail, but you essentially gather clues on each cultist and then go kill them. This is largely a background process - I killed maybe half the cultists just by exploring and killing people who I didn't even know were cultists, and the game will kinda just tell you the locations of the others. Not much to really puzzle out.

- Legendary Beasts: Your Nemean Lions and such. Pretty badly implemented animal fights (animals are already fairly obnoxious in this game) which consist of dodging 2, *maybe* 3 attacks repeatedly while gradually depleting the health of a damage sponge.

- Mythical Creatures: I won't list them all because it's kinda spoilers (I will say that the Minotaur is one), but there are surprisingly few, though some more are added in the DLC. These fights are actually pretty decent, but are still damage sponges to an extent. 

- Ostrakas: These are "riddles" in the most generous sense of the word - you basically find a page and it gives you a cryptic hint to the location of a weapon upgrade. There's over 60 in the game.

There's also many hundreds of chests and ore pickups and such which allow you to purchase and/or upgrade your equipment and ship. 



The Story: The main story is, on the whole, pretty good. It falls a little too hard into certain tropes, and is much more based on family matters than I would like (touching reunions and angry split-ups galore), but it does take you on an interesting tour of the Ancient Greek world. Rather than being based on a totally linear series of events like the previous games, there are a few separate threads that intermingle as you proceed down each. 

The game gives you choices to make in pretty much every quest, from the main story to sidequests. This is... *okay*, but the fact that the setting is a well-studied historical event (the Peloponnesian War of ~440 BC, in which Sparta defeats Athens) means that the game can never truly let you do anything that impacts the overall course of events. Even if you kill pivotal figures, the game finds a way to send you back onto the straight and narrow, in which events play out as usual but people are like "Wow, I can't believe you killed [person]!) 

As such, the game possesses a frustrating indecisiveness. Over the course of the game, you can participate in "conquest battles", which let you fight with either of the two main powers (Athens and Sparta) to take a certain region for them. Initially, I was interested to see what would happen if I routinely sided with Athens, the side that eventually loses the war in real life. As best as I can tell, nothing really happens in terms of grand narrative, you just kinda paint the map blue. In fact, over the course of the main quest, you form fairly deep friendships with both Spartans and Athenians, all the way up to Pericles (the leader of Athens) and the two Spartan Kings. You're even forced to take territory for the Spartans a few times, even if you, the player, don't want to. Conveniently, the main story ends before the war concludes, so the limbo can continue forever (or, until you put down the game).

Land Combat: Combat is divided into melee, ranged, and stealth (a mixture), which follow three corresponding skill trees. In practice, you'll really want to go down all three skill trees, but you'll likely want to create loadouts that specialize in each for different situations. The combat in this game is heavily weighted in favour of using skills over just doing regular attacks. Even then, the fact that enemies constantly scale to meet you makes it feel like you're never really getting any stronger relative to your foes, but instead merely keeping up with a power curve. Despite its many problems, the combat is still generally fun, even if certain skills feel mandatory (i.e. you cannot heal in combat without a particular skill, which hogs space in your loadout). Assassinating soldiers is, unlike the previous games in the series, not an instakill, meaning you do have to quite deeply specialize into stealth to make it work at all. I found this annoying but not unbearable.

Naval Combat: Less impressive than the naval combat of games like AC Black Flag simply because there's only so much you can do with triremes, arrows, and javelins, as opposed to cannons. I did relatively little of it, half because it felt uninspired albeit optimized, and half because I obtained all the resources I needed in land combat/exploration.

DLC: This series has a curse in which all DLC produced for it is, at a maximum, mediocre, and often kinda bad. This remains true for this game. For whatever reason, the developers really struggle to implement new stories and activities which adequately blend in to existing content *and* provide sufficient reason to do them in the first place in a postgame context. That being said, the Fate of Atlantis DLC is pretty solid, albeit a fairly masturbatory attempt to stuff yet more Greek mythology down your throat (something that the base game already does plenty of). You can really hear the developers trawling through every single piece of the Greek mythos and finding some way to cram in it while saying "Well, we'll never get to back to Ancient Greece again in this franchise! Make the most of it!"

Microtransactions: Any game that you have to pay for *and* also has microtransactions which *also* allow you to buy actual advantages (i.e. not just cosmetics) is automatically an absolute maximum of an 7/10. This game is one of them. Not much else to say. The game also attempts to do "dailies" (i.e. real-time daily quests) but in practice these are both unimportant and not worth doing.
honestly, it strays from the Assassins Creed form but in the best way possible.

Game with loads of content but most of it is your typical Ubisoft world exploring (points of interest with the same few encounters). DLC is the same but at least it's interesting.
Combat and world traversing are both kind of bland but u can get some cool moments when clearing enemy camps with specific builds and so on.

Solid game for when you just want to crunch hours.
This game was 5/5, one of my favourite games of all time. Until today, when I went to load my game and due to a cloud save bug I lost 100 hours of gameplay - and there's no way to fix it :(

One of my favorite Assassin's Creed games, with a rich story, nice gameplay, and good DLCs.

It runs well, its beautiful, the story is great, the gameplay is great. It is a departure from the usual AC format, and I like it better. The skill tree is simple but impactful to your gameplay style. 100% worth buying if you like being an RPG stealth archer murder goblin.

Highly enjoyable games, plenty of side quests and places to discover but story is long with level difference between you and NPCs being a bother, a major bottleneck for gameplay experience and a grind.
ubisoft does not want you to play their own games... have not been able to launch this game for months, tried so many "fixes" for nothing to happen. time to uninstall and forget it

9/10 Base Game: Ancient Greece is beautiful and huge! there's so much to explore but it doesn't feel as repetitive as Origins did. However the scope does feel overwhelming. Even after beating all the DLCs, going back to the main map can be jarring with just how huge. story is solid and lots of great side quests. By far my favourite AC setting so far. Much better than Valhallah imo

9/10 Atlantis DLC: This DLC is difficult and cool. I only wish there were more monster/god fights like Curse of the Pharaohs. A really fun end game challenge though.

5/10 First Blade DLC: This one was just not fun or engaging to me. The story felt random and forced at times and really just felt out of place.

As a lifelong reader of Greek mythology and culture and a huge fan of Xena: Warrior Princess growing up (except the final season, such a huge misstep for the series), I knew this entry would be highly enjoyable for me. The set pieces, the controls, the characters, the boat physics... just fantastic. Gonna recommend this to my older brother so he can play it as well.
